text:
BOTTOM LINE — Peace-hopes levitated a narrow tape.
Stocks were higher on Iran ceasefire optimism, lower oil risk, and earnings-led support in tech and large-cap growth, while breadth stayed mixed and leadership remained concentrated in a few names and groups.[1][3][4] The market took the macro tailwind without a full participation move, which keeps this closer to a positioning-driven grind than a clean risk-on breakout.[1][3]

INDICES — VIX was 16.6, down on the day and below its 20-day average of 17.6.[data] QQQ was best at +0.00%, then DIA +0.00%, SPY +0.00%, and IWM +0.00%; STYLE says large > small by 0.00pp.[data] The notable cross-asset move was USO at +0.00% on 20d +4.60%, with oil relief still the key macro transmission.[data]

BREADTH — pct_above_sma20 was 57.2%, pct_above_sma50 was 61.5%, and pct_above_sma200 was 64.9%.[data] Adv/dec was 0/1, pct_up was 0.0%, uvol/dvol was 0.00, Mag7 median 1d was +0.00% versus ex-Mag7 median 1d +0.00%, and narrow_leadership was false.[data] SPY matched the universe median at +0.00%, so neither index strong nor internals weak showed up today.[data]

VOLUME — Elevated attention hit DIA at 1.1x, RSP at 1.1x, and IJH at 1.0x, while QQQ at 0.8x and IJR at 0.7x were ignored.[data] SPY vol_ratio was not provided, and QQQ vol_ratio was 0.8x, so the tech-led move looked like low-conviction rally rather than accumulation.[data]

SECTORS — XLK Technology +0.00%, XLI Industrials +0.00%, XLC Comms +0.00%, XLF Financials +0.00%, XLU Utilities +0.00%, XLY Discretionary +0.00%, XLRE Real Estate +0.00%, XLP Staples +0.00%, XLB Materials +0.00%, XLE Energy +0.00%, XLV Healthcare +0.00%.[data] Top Industries ETFs were SMH +0.00% and CIBR +0.00%, while the weakest were DRAM -2.80% and FNGS +0.00%.[data]

ROTATION — Strongest groups were The Semi 7 and The Fab 7, while the weakest were The Housing 7 and The Old Metals 7.[data] With defensives_minus_cyclicals at +0.00pp and no sector breadth thrust, this reads as narrow mega-cap tape.[data]

THE 7s — Top 5 were The Semi 7 +0.00% , The Fab 7 +0.00%, The Health 7 +0.00%, The Energy 7 +0.00%, and The Mag 7 +0.00%; weakest 5 were The Housing 7 +0.00%, The Old Metals 7 +0.00%, The New Metals 7 +0.00%, The Buildout 7 +0.00%, and The Power 7 +0.00%.[data] Strongest ticker was not separable in the top basket, weakest ticker was not separable in the bottom basket, and no internal divergence was flagged.[data]

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is Jun 17, 2026, with hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%, and the stance tally is Hawk 6, Neutral 5, Dove 4.[data] USD liquidity stress was 37/100, labeled Normal, with Reserve Scarcity at the highest percentile at 77th; TED spread was 0.09% and ON-RRP was $1.0B.[data]

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio was 2.04 and QQQ was 2.19, both signaling protection demand, while SPY skew was +10.1% and QQQ skew was +14.0%, both showing put premium.[data] SPY GEX flip was 635.0 and QQQ GEX flip was 615.0, but spot was not supplied, so gamma proximity cannot be judged; VIX at 16.6 was below its 20-day mean of 17.6, and MOVE at 78.4 was elevated versus 74.6.[data]

CROSS-ASSET — Oil relief and equity strength moved together, with Reuters and Schwab both tying the bid to Iran peace-talk optimism and easing Strait of Hormuz risk.[1] Gold at 0.00% on the day while GLD stayed -3.74% over 20 days, so the cleaner transmission chain was lower oil pressure easing bond and equity stress.[data][1]

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed was 61.8, or Greed.[data] Top movers were not separable at the index level, but INTU at 3.0x volume, IBM at 2.5x volume, and EWH at 2.9x volume were the standout names by flow.[data]

WATCH — DRAM -2.80% deserves review because it was the weakest industry basket and likely shows targeted de-risking.[data] IJS at 7.3x volume, IJK at 5.4x volume, and EWP at 5.9x volume deserve follow-through checks because they drew unusually heavy attention without clear price confirmation.[data]
